result.summary: Grafana exposes two developer HTTP APIs—a tenant/instance Grafana HTTP API and the grafana.com Grafana Cloud API—and a Grafana MCP server; auth is via service account tokens, Cloud Access Policy tokens, or on-prem Basic auth for the instance HTTP API/MCP server.
result.description: Grafana provides observability software and services for dashboards, metrics, logs, traces, profiling, alerting, and incident response, including managed Grafana Cloud and self-hosted Grafana. Its products let teams collect, query, visualize, and operate on telemetry and related operational data.

cloud_access_policy_token.label: Grafana Cloud Access Policy token
cloud_access_policy_token.setup: In Grafana Cloud, create a Cloud access policy and token from either the Cloud Portal or Grafana Administration. The easiest path is documented at [Create access policies and tokens](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/security-and-account-management/authentication-and-permissions/access-policies/create-access-policies/). For Cloud API use, create an organization or stack access policy with the scopes you need, then create a token for that policy. Send it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

grafana_service_account_token.label: Grafana service account token
grafana_service_account_token.setup: Create a service account in Grafana, then add a token to it. The UI path is documented at [Service accounts](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/administration/service-accounts/) and [HTTP API authentication](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/developer-resources/api-reference/http-api/authentication/): go to **Administration** → **Users and access** → **Service Accounts**, create the service account, then create a token. Use the token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. In Grafana Cloud, this is the supported auth method for the instance HTTP API.

grafana_basic_auth.label: Grafana username/password for basic auth
grafana_basic_auth.setup: For Grafana OSS/Enterprise only, use a Grafana user account with basic authentication enabled. The docs at [HTTP API authentication](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/developer-resources/api-reference/http-api/authentication/) show basic auth is available on prem only. Use your Grafana username and password with standard HTTP Basic authentication against your instance.
